2024-05-25.abstract: In 2016, Britain voted for Brexit and America for Trump, heralding a populist wave. Both countries face pivotal elections this year — but their paths appear to have diverged.

2024-05-25.title: Xi Jinping’s Recipe for Total Control: An Army of Eyes and Ears
2024-05-25.abstract: Reviving a Mao-era surveillance campaign, the authorities are tracking residents, schoolchildren and businesses to forestall any potential unrest.

2024-05-25.title: Hundreds Feared Dead in Papua New Guinea Landslide
2024-05-25.abstract: Nearly 4,000 people live in villages that were engulfed, a local official said. Unstable debris was complicating search and rescue efforts in a rural part of the Pacific nation.

2024-05-25.title: One of the Deadliest Jobs in Mexico: Running for Office
2024-05-25.abstract: The assassination of Gisela Gaytán shocked Mexico. She was among dozens of aspirants for public office killed in recent months.

2024-05-25.title: Why Are So Many Mexican Election Candidates Getting Killed?
2024-05-25.abstract: Across Mexico, dozens of candidates, their relatives and party members have been targeted in violent attacks before next month’s general election.

2024-05-26.title: Free Food? Modi Makes Sure Every Indian Knows Whom to Thank for It.
2024-05-26.abstract: India’s welfare programs improve lives. Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s party uses its vast machinery to ensure the handouts also create loyal voters.

2024-05-26.title: Amateur Historians Heard Tales of a Lost Tudor Palace. Then, They Dug It Up.
2024-05-26.abstract: In a small English village, a group of dedicated locals has unearthed the remains of a long-vanished palace that had been home to Henry VIII’s grandmother.

2024-05-26.title: The Hamas Chief and the Israeli Who Saved His Life
2024-05-26.abstract: In an Israeli prison infirmary, a Jewish dentist came to the aid of a desperately ill Hamas inmate. Years later, the prisoner became a mastermind of the Oct. 7 attack.

2024-05-26.title: Optimistic About the War in Ukraine, Putin Unleashes a Purge at Home
2024-05-26.abstract: Despite years of criticism, President Vladimir V. Putin has only now changed his defense minister and allowed high-level corruption arrests.

2024-05-26.title: A Public Park or Private Spa: A City Debates the Future of an Island Oasis
2024-05-26.abstract: Officials in Toronto and many residents ask why the province of Ontario is turning a former waterfront park over to an Austrian spa operator.

2024-05-27.title: This Island Wants to Round Up Its Wild Goats. Catching Them Won’t Be Easy.
2024-05-27.abstract: The ruminants outnumber humans on the Mediterranean island of Alicudi 6 to 1 (maybe more). Officials have devised a plan to catch the goats and send them to adoptive homes elsewhere.

2024-05-27.title: Spying Arrests Send Chill Through Britain’s Thriving Hong Kong Community
2024-05-27.abstract: Tens of thousands of Hong Kongers have resettled in the U.K. since 2021, among them prominent pro-democracy activists. China has not forgotten them.

2024-05-27.title: The Deadly Prelude to South Africa’s First Free Elections
2024-05-27.abstract: The vote in 1994 was a time of hope — but in the weeks before, the country came close to the abyss. A photographer remembers what he witnessed.

2024-05-27.title: Papua New Guinea Landslide Has Buried 2,000 People, Officials Say
2024-05-27.abstract: Just getting to survivors has proved to be an enormous challenge, with a blocked highway and unstable ground “posing ongoing danger” to rescue workers.

2024-05-27.title: Israeli Airstrike at Tent Camp Kills Dozens, Officials Say
2024-05-27.abstract: The Israeli military said the attack was aimed at a Hamas compound, and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called the civilian deaths “a tragic accident.”

2024-05-28.title: Leaflet by Leaflet, a Few Aging Activists Fight India’s Tide of Bigotry
2024-05-28.abstract: As politicians stoke religious hatred online, these veterans of social causes deliver their messages in person: “Talk to each other. Don’t let anyone divide you.”

2024-05-28.title: South Africa’s Young Democracy Leaves Its Young Voters Disillusioned
2024-05-28.abstract: We spoke to South Africans who grew up in the three decades since the country overthrew apartheid and held its first free election about their lives and plans to vote — or not — in this week's pivotal election.

2024-05-28.title: Cyclone Remal Uproots Trees, Rips Down Power Lines in Bangladesh
2024-05-28.abstract: People braved high winds and heavy rain to clear debris as the storm tore through the region.

2024-05-28.title: Cyclone Remal Tears Through India and Bangladesh, Killing at Least 23
2024-05-28.abstract: The storm, the first of the season, hit the South Asian countries after weeks of intense heat. Millions were left without power.

2024-05-28.title: War-Weary Iraqis Feel for Gaza, but Fear Spread of the Conflict
2024-05-28.abstract: Iraqis want to help Palestinians but say they are still recovering from their own wars and fear playing host to a proxy fight between Iran and the United States.

2024-05-29.title: Haiti Names New Prime Minister to Try to Lead Country Out of Crisis
2024-05-29.abstract: Garry Conille is taking on the office just ahead of the arrival of a Kenyan-led international police force charged with helping restore order to the violence-torn nation.

2024-05-29.title: Briefly Open to a Fair-Looking Election, Venezuela Reverses Course Again
2024-05-29.abstract: Officials rescinded an invitation to E.U. observers for the presidential vote in July, in another sign that Nicolás Maduro is unlikely to cede power regardless of the result.

2024-05-29.title: Hold the French Fries! Paris Olympics Chart a New Gastronomic Course.
2024-05-29.abstract: The environment will come first as France tries to revitalize the global image of its cuisine.

2024-05-29.title: Can South Africa’s Opposition Parties Break Through?
2024-05-29.abstract: A record 51 parties are competing to unseat the long-ruling African National Congress in the national election on Wednesday. Here’s why it won’t be easy.

2024-05-29.title: South Africa’s Black Elites Sour on the President They Championed
2024-05-29.abstract: In pivotal elections on Wednesday, President Cyril Ramaphosa and his African National Congress party are struggling to keep the support of Black middle- and upper-class voters.

2024-05-30.title: Hong Kong Convicts Democracy Activists in Largest National Security Trial
2024-05-30.abstract: As part of China’s crackdown on even peaceful opposition, a court in Hong Kong convicted 14 people, who now face prison time along with dozens of others.

2024-05-30.title: What the I.C.J. Ruling Actually Means for Israel’s Offensive in Rafah
2024-05-30.abstract: There is a substantial consensus among legal experts that Israel cannot continue its current Rafah offensive without violating the court’s order.

2024-05-30.title: Singapore Releases Initial Report on Deadly Turbulence That Hit Flight
2024-05-30.abstract: A man died and more than 70 other people were injured last week when a flight to Singapore from London hit sudden turbulence.

2024-05-30.title: New Scandal Engulfs South Korean Leader in Wake of Marine’s Death
2024-05-30.abstract: President Yoon Suk Yeol has been accused of intervening in an inquiry into an accidental death during a peacetime search and rescue operation.

2024-05-30.title: ‘Not Everything Was Bad’: Saluting the Mercedes of Eastern Europe and a Communist Past
2024-05-30.abstract: A festival of classic cars from the communist era brings out some nostalgia in eastern Germany for pre-unification days, although the abuses that occurred behind the Iron Curtain aren’t forgotten.

2024-05-31.title: Why I Love the Sydney Opera House
2024-05-31.abstract: The art is accessible, the attendees cut across the social spectrum and the architecture beguiles.

2024-05-31.title: U.S. and Chinese Defense Chiefs Hold First In-Person Talks Since 2022
2024-05-31.abstract: Lloyd J. Austin III met his Chinese counterpart as both countries try to show they can manage their disputes over Taiwan and the South China Sea.

2024-05-31.title: As U.S. Shifts Policy on Striking Into Russia, Kharkiv Is Hit Again
2024-05-31.abstract: After weeks of entreaties, Ukraine won permission to hit targets inside Russia with American-made weapons, a tactic that it says will help it defend territory in the northeast.

2024-05-31.title: After Trump’s Conviction, a Wary World Waits for the Fallout
2024-05-31.abstract: Already braced for uncertainty about the U.S. election, countries in Europe and Asia are now even more unclear about the future of American diplomacy.

2024-05-31.title: A Matter of Survival as South Asia’s Heat Wave Reaches 127 Degrees
2024-05-31.abstract: Pakistan and India are sweltering. For laborers, not working because of the extreme temperatures can mean not eating.
